items.criterion: buildDeleteShotGroupMutation is moved into a dedicated pure shot-group-commands module and TimelineEditor imports it from there
Pinned shot-group membership and mode transitions are built through pure command helpers rather than ad hoc mutation assembly in multiple UI hooks
Final-video shot-group operations still preserve current external behavior after the refactor
The refactor adds or updates focused coverage for updateToLatestVideo and preserves useExternalDrop pinned-group behavior
Focused video-editor tests covering TimelineEditor, useTimelineCommit, and pinned-group behavior pass after the refactor
The full src/tools/video-editor test suite passes after the refactor
shot-group-commands module has no React dependencies
No new TimelineEditMutation variants are introduced
Manual smoke test: delete a shot via the context menu and undo it in the video editor

rationale: critique flag addressed directly: plan now requires updateToLatestVideo coverage and useExternalDrop validation
signals_assessment: Forced proceed override applied by the orchestrator.
orchestrator_guidance: Force-proceed override applied. Proceed to finalize.

signals.idea: Light megaplan and execution for the video editor pinned-shot-group architecture. Understand every place pinned shot groups couple to timeline clip state, design and implement the right command abstraction so shot-group operations are atomic and consistent, fix the current delete/undo regression structurally rather than ad hoc, and add appropriate regression coverage without overreaching into unrelated timeline behavior.

resolved_flags.concern: Pinned-group sync suppression: the preferred boolean `isRestoringRef` alternative will not actually suppress the post-undo sync, because `restoreSnapshot()` would set and clear the ref before the `useEffect` triggered by `commitData(setData)` runs. The guard has to survive past the render boundary, not just the synchronous restore call.
resolved_flags.resolution: `useTimelineHistory.restoreSnapshot()` commits synchronously at `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineHistory.ts:158-170`, and `commitData()` updates React state immediately at `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineCommit.ts:156-203`. `usePinnedGroupSync()` only checks its skip condition inside the later timeout/effect path at `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/usePinnedShotGroups.ts:175-210,201-210`, so an immediately-reset boolean ref would already be false when the sync actually runs.

resolved_flags.concern: Pinned-group sync plumbing: step 3 says to wire the suppression ref through `useTimelineState.ts` "where both `useTimelineHistory` and `usePinnedGroupSync` are composed", but in this repo `usePinnedGroupSync` is mounted directly in `TimelineEditor.tsx`, not in `useTimelineState`. The file ownership is still incomplete unless the plan explicitly includes the `TimelineEditor` call site or another real path into that hook.
resolved_flags.resolution: `useTimelineState()` instantiates history at `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineState.ts:65-68` and never calls `usePinnedGroupSync()`. The actual sync hook call is `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineEditor.tsx:592-599`.

resolved_flags.concern: Pinned-group sync verification: the plan's main regression assertion (`delete -> undo` does not trigger a follow-up sync mutation) cannot be proven in `useTimelineHistory.test.ts` or a pure `shot-group-commands.test.ts`, because those harnesses do not mount `usePinnedGroupSync` or expose an `applyEdit` spy. The existing sync tests live in `useClipEditing.test.ts`, and the planned validation run does not include that suite.
resolved_flags.resolution: `useTimelineHistory.test.ts` renders only `useTimelineHistory({ dataRef, commitData })` at `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineHistory.test.ts:114-117`. The current `usePinnedGroupSync` coverage is in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipEditing.test.ts:644-976`. Step 4's proposed test run mentions `useTimelineHistory.test.ts`, `useTimelineCommit.test.tsx`, and `TimelineEditor.test.ts`, but not the hook test file that already exercises sync behavior.

signals.loop_summary: Iteration 1. Weighted score trajectory: 0. Plan deltas: n/a. Recurring critiques: 0. Resolved flags: 3. Open significant flags: 0.
